rationale.R1.1: The open-ended delegation invites assessment of the thesis framing, contribution claim, and conceptual positioning of DialogueProb.
rationale.R1.2: Evaluating what one thinks of the artifact centrally includes checking whether the argument from semantics to inference benefits to system contribution coheres.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes empirical and practical claims about benchmarks, calibration, wall-clock cost, and posterior estimates that require alignment with reported evidence.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact explicitly foregrounds firstness and contrast with prior frameworks, making novelty assessment a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation asks for general reaction to a research-writing artifact, sentence-level clarity, flow, and register are expected parts of the evaluation.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of an open-ended opinion request must disclose uncertainty about unstated evaluation criteria and missing surrounding paper context.
rationale.RX.2: The strong firstness, calibration, and equivalence claims require attention to avoiding overclaiming.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must remain within projection of responsibility rather than performing the requested critique or revising the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid enabling misleading research claims or unsupported publication-facing assertions.
rationale.RX.5: The responsibility assignment should remain traceable to the delegation wording and specific claims in the artifact.
